speaker: MATT
raw_text: Thank you for your interest in our show and our audience, so for those of you who are interested, Loot Crate is a really cool service where once a month they bring you themed boxes that have mystery contents within that are part of that theme. All generally pop culture and geek-centric.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Welcome to tonight's episode of Critical Role, where a bunch of us nerdy-ass voice actors get together and play Dungeons & Dragons. Tonight, apologies for the slight technical issues, but we're back and we're going to be playing through tonight's adventure. Before that gets started, let's go ahead and get through a couple of quick announcements. First and foremost, it's our third week of our Loot Crate sponsorship. Thank you very much. (cheering)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I like it. You guys, having just finished this stroke of a conversation with Zanror in the Margrave's homestead, the dawn light is now beginning to pour over the mountain range in the distance. The day has officially begun. Zanror has stepped out and has begun to speak with a few of his trusted Herd members to stand down for the day, letting people know that the battle has been postponed for the time being while a plan is to be enacted, prepared for, and then sprung the following day. The rest of you guys have this day to prepare however you see fit. So I ask you: what would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't like the way he said that. Why'd you have to say it like that? It's just a black dragon!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What else am I going to say?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Why the dramatic pauses?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Because it's fun.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You used the word 'somehow'.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So! After taking the better part of about three hours scouring the town, most of the remaining villagers--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll tell you what. Go ahead and roll an investigation check, Percy, if you don't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we help him at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll say if you want to go ahead and aid to that, take advantage on that, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank god. Yeah, no, not very good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What'd you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I rolled a six.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: With advantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Three hours, so, from what he's doing with the aid of people that aided him, which would be Vex asking about, the few folks that have any experience with any sort of blacksmithy are the folks that make horseshoes, and that's the extent of the complication they can create. So they can attempt to, with your aid, craft some springs. They have the materials to do very basic metalwork and ironwork, but the people you encounter, two folks in particular, they're both a little confused by your overall design and are waiting for you to lead them in a specific direction.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. I'll tell you what. Go ahead and roll an investigation check, Percy, if you don't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Can we help him at all?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'll say if you want to go ahead and aid to that, take advantage on that, Percy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Thank god. Yeah, no, not very good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What'd you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I rolled a six.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: With advantage?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So! After taking the better part of about three hours scouring the town, most of the remaining villagers--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He took three hours doing that?!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I'm not saying you're going nuts. You are a little stretched for supplies and manpower based on the circumstances in Westruun for a complicated task, but it's fairly possible, but it will pretty much take the entirety of your day and most of your night to prepare this. You'll be able to squeeze by with this, with the construction of the device, and have maybe just enough room for-- I'll let you squeeze in a long rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm just going to take them and I'm going to start building one and see if they can follow suit then. Three springs will be more than enough for this design.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: How big a spring?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I would say, how tall is this dragon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roughly 30 to 40 feet, depending.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: About a two-to-three-foot spring, then. Three two-to-three-foot springs made of solid iron. Just enough to lift the system and toss. And it's just, you make them like chainmail. It's a process that exists. I'm not going nuts.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 17. Yeah, dragons have the pieces you imagine they would have in the right vicinity. Their stomach is quite acidic, as it has been known to dissolve some of the hardiest of creatures, let alone break down some basic minerals and materials. So it's not a happy place to be, but it's not like instantaneous dissolution. It will hurt. Also know that the breath weapon, the acid that a black dragon, which uses acid as a breath weapon in a stream in front of it, much like your lightning bolt, does not come from its stomach. That acid is much more toxic, concentrated, and comes from a separate part of its body, a gland, a breathing mechanism that projects it outward.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I slap him on the cheek a little bit and say: you are a fucking madman, but I am glad you're here.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm glad to be with you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And I love you. And I give him a playful slap across the face.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Six points of damage. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Unexpected, sneak attack. 7d6 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, so I'm going to go talk to Vex. An expert in dragons I hope?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm an expert on dragons.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: And ask her how dragons work, how their bodies work.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you mean?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Where they're vulnerable, where they make the acid, where they have lungs, where they have stomachs, how many stomachs? Are they like a cow with eight stomachs or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and make an intelligence check with advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Cows have eight stomachs?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They've got like two or three.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Shut up! 17.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You would know the direction he went, and thankfully in the awkward still-quiet that is the center of the town now that it's missing most of its populace, and those that are there are acknowledging the tension of what might be coming, and many of them are now packing up and leaving and getting out of the town, evacuating when they've been notified of the possible coming battle. The sound of metal clangs in the distance, and the gathering of materials from some of the rubble is not too difficult to discern over time. Takes you about half an hour or so to locate him.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm very charming.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Now you're not here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You told me to leave! You took off your clothes. I'm Liam, I'm not Vax, what're you talking about?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you planning on doing something?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're all planning on doing something, and I don't think that my part of the plan is any stupider than yours will be. So. Good luck to you. I'll keep your armor safe. Hopefully we can trade back. And if not, you can peel it off my dead body.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: As long as you're not melted from all the acid.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to attune to my frostbrand sword.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so you'll be putting aside the mace of disruption and taking on the frostband instead. Okay. Cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit, I go running after Percival. I'm going to go find Percival.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Well. Does that take an hour, or do I catch up with him pretty quick?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. And you've been here before, you know the Iron Stocks, although you weren't sure if it was being used as still a prison. And seemed loosely, just throwing a handful of people in for use later on that never quite came to fruition.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Are there any still in there? Or are they all clear?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, there's plenty of prisoners still in there, we didn't touch the prison, mostly."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hm. All right, thank you. (whispers) What was Reginald's daughter's name?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if I wrote it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I wrote it down.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'd like to make my way over to the Iron Stocks.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I've only been in here for about a week. Got in an altercation in my store when these brutes started walking in. Threw me in here, didn't even let me take my spellbook."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah, you're very welcome! Hold on. Is there anybody else in this hallway before I give away my last bit of food?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You hear two voices simultaneously down the end of the hallway come in, one of which is a very weak female voice, the other is a very loud kind of-- "Hey, bring me food this way, please!" The other one is like, "Please, I ask of you, I'm starving." And you see these two hands come out of the two cells in the distance.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right, I'm going to pick a number between one and ten. Ready? Go.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There's an awkward pause. "Three?" "Seven! Definitely seven!"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I lean over to Abjurist Noja, I say, I actually don't know any numbers, so. Y'know what, we're going to deliberate and we'll be with you in just a moment. (whispers) Abjurist, how old y--, why are you in here, who put you in here?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Ah! All righty. So. After spending the good part of an hour or so clamoring through, just throwing bookshelves over and (crashing) taking the desk and actually throwing it onto its back and pulling drawers out, this is Grog's method of investigation here, you're just--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How long has it been since you've been to Emon?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Few months?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You got any loved ones there?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Why?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know, just asking, like, do you want me to take a note from you to them or something, you know.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No, if you can release me, I'd be more than happy to take it myself if you don't mind."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, I'm still deliberating, sorry. Enjoy the food that you snuck in your jacket pocket. And I go back towards the front desk area. And I try and find any-- is there a lever on the wall to release the cells or can I look for keys?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You-- make an investigation check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fuck me.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Your fate is in excellent hands. Oh shit, you're not dead! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Minus two for a 17!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: I mean, it is one solid piece that has been looped twice, but it has been-- the iron itself has been bent around itself to make it nice and tight, form-fit on one end. Go ahead and make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Amelia, if you would, please stay with me. Funny, I believe I heard of a man named Reginald. Very brave. Not sure where he is, but we're going to get you out of the city, just stay put for a moment.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Okay."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right, you, you salty bastard, what's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Old Davin. What's your name?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Lord Grog. Emphasis on the Lord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Lord Grog."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Lord of?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: The Vales of Strongjaw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'll take it, that's fine. Get me out, please?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Right. Oh, keys. Can I take the keyring and can I pry the metal keyring-- is it like one solid piece or is it the kind that I could maybe bend it open?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Not yet. But at this time there's an awkward pause and now you're like, don't have any more conversation. But you still have to finish the-- about a minute later, he's sitting there going, "Come on, come on, come on." Eventually-- tink!-- it opens up. "Yes!" And he pushes it open as you pull it open.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, oh." And he thinks to himself for a moment. "I would have to say my weapon of choice would be Bloodvine ale. Aye."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Bloodvine ale?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Aye, if we're picking poison, that's my favorite."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, no, my-- He's a dwarf, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Obviously we are brothers of the beard. Like, to fight with, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, that kills, eh?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, stab, poke, enchant, whatever.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Once upon a time I was quite a duelist, so if you got something pointy and prickling I can spear 'em with the best of them."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do have a Greatsword of Frenzy, but it's a two-handed weapon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Ah, that's not my preference."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I do also have one regular, just a regular longsword. About the best I have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yeah, that'll do, certainly, I'll take a longsword."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: All right, so. Have I got the door open yet?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Oh, it's about-- just due east of where the temples are, about 25, 30 minutes up that way."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Look for Noja's book, look for Noja's book.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, yeah, well. Noja, Noja, is there a location where your spellbook was last? (belches)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "They took it from me when they arrested me. I don't know where they would have put it."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, well, goliaths are awfully fond of books. Let's go find out, shall we? And we go back towards where I guess everybody last was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. On the way there, Noja's like, "I do have one of my older spellbooks, wouldn't have all of my materials, but that would be back at my shop if we ever have a chance to stop by."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well, we could go there now if you'd like.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Certainly."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How far is it from here? I forget, it's been a few years.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah, the attunement takes about an hour or so, half an hour to an hour.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we're like really important.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we know a lot of people.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: So I'm going to take him to find his spellbook, because I feel like he could be a great--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I don't recall this person.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You don't?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Weren't you with us, Percy?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, Percy met him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Was I?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, remember the guy who saved us at the first Winter's Crest festival?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yes, no, he was quite lovely.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he's very nice.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I found him. I'm going to get him a spellbook. Does anybody want to come with, or are you all good?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I'm about to dig a trench, so I'll pass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keyleth, where are you right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Heading northeast towards, northwest, northwest. Sorry. It's noon, it's hard to tell in the sky right now. Northwest.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you with us, Pike?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Pike's with us.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm done attuning?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "It only lasts for a few hours, depending, the magic isn't perfect, but it does make you smaller. This other one, this vial contains a liquid that will make you quite speedier. It will increase your metabolism for a short time, the world around you seems to slow, it's quite effective."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yellow with black swirls, and then a clear potion that--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, he'll just tell us, I assume. Sir, we're about to embark on a deadly adventure to try to clear this town so that you can once again live here safely. Might we trouble you to borrow these potions if they can be in some way helpful to us?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "You've done me great service by getting me out of that hellhole. You are welcome to take these with you for now. I guess I have to start from scratch..."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you know what they are?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Oh, yes, I'm sorry, I'm sorry." And he takes the vials and looks at them. "Ah, this is a Potion of Diminution. It shrinks you to a smaller size."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, I could be small! Oh, wait.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah. The two of you guys, make one more investigation check, this'll signify the next few hours of looking for the things you want.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. About that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Orn? Orn Blackfoot? Gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, figured that much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He has peaced.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: He left.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. You guys take a moment, and when you start deciding what to do, and you look around and he's just gone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I thought you had him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I thought you had him. I clearly said when you came up, watch the guy with the black feet. What'd you do?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was watching you, you have disgusting black feet. You walk around barefoot, you're disgusting.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I really can't argue with that. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Well. Whatever. I saved a life, go me. I'll take it later. Yeah, so can we go back to the prison, not to take away from everything else, and maybe look for chains, like some hooks, maybe some broken bars?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 23. As the evening comes into about ten or 11 o'clock at night, after hours of aching work, sweat beads dripping down the foreheads of the three of you, forcing the furnace to work overtime. The room's heat and darkness eventually almost puts you to a point of exhaustion and passing out. However, you do manage to complete the springs. They seem to be functional, the structure of your trap is set. We shall see on the morrow if it is as functional as you hope it is. You do take a point of exhaustion through the work that will consist throughout the next day.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, so, initial designs and describing with your lackeys, for lack of a better term, that are helping you develop these springs, you manage to explain, lay out, and develop the proper materials, the process, and they seem to acknowledge and understand what you're trying to do, and you guys begin the work. Second check is going to be with your dexterity modifier.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Oh yeah, baby.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: No one can help him on these things?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're not there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You spend the better part of the day helping them take the metal, strengthen it, bind it, pulling it into the springs that are required. You complete one, you get partway through the second one. Make a final check if you don't mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: With dex?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is also with dex, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's the dexterity, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So yeah, that's right, 23.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Disadvantage on ability checks. So, essentially, any skill checks or ability checks you make-- not attacks or saves, but ability checks you make for the next 24 hours will be at disadvantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: With dex?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: This is also with dex, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: That's 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it's the dexterity, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So yeah, that's right, 23.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 23. As the evening comes into about ten or 11 o'clock at night, after hours of aching work, sweat beads dripping down the foreheads of the three of you, forcing the furnace to work overtime. The room's heat and darkness eventually almost puts you to a point of exhaustion and passing out. However, you do manage to complete the springs. They seem to be functional, the structure of your trap is set. We shall see on the morrow if it is as functional as you hope it is. You do take a point of exhaustion through the work that will consist throughout the next day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay. What does a point of exhaustion do?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 15. You circle for a while and keep watch. So far, nothing seems to have caught your attention that's out of the ordinary. About half an hour passes before you see a group of civilians, along with Percy, wheeling on carts this large ramshackle construction made of support beams from houses, metal joints that were used as basic construction elements, as well as these large metallic springs on multiple sides with hooks and elements of a chain-based net that has been piecemealed together. Eventually you guys bring it over. You clear out the area.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: In theory, it has to be laid down underneath all the treasure so that it just is nothing, and then we put everything on top, and then basically it's set, and then pins get pulled so that it's set. And you don't disturb shit again.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hey Percy, we're ready to fly in the trap whenever you are.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes, let's do that, gentlemen. Let's move it. Good work, ladies and gentlemen.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: While you guys set the trap, I'm going to turn into a raven. My job is done here. I'm going to just go and circle while they're setting the trap and setting the stage and do patrol and make sure we're not being watched.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, make a perception check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why this creepy music?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's random, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Why is he doing his eyebrows up and down?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Because the fucking dragon's going to see us right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, sorry. 15.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: 31. You guys take a good portion of the next hour or so, under her guidance and tutelage, hiding this entire trap, getting dirt, the top layer of sand to cover it so it looks like it hasn't been recently disturbed--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So you set it up and away, laying the sides down, precariously putting the pins in, holding it in an open position. You begin to take dirt, dust, and gathering up the area, trying to hide it to the best of your ability. Who is in charge of camouflaging this piece?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go ranger, go ranger, go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "Which dice will I use? There's no way to choose."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I aid her in this bit of camouflage?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You want to sing me some inspiration?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I inspire her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, what do you want to sing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) R-r-ranger, turn and face the strange! R-r-ranger, looks like you're going to have to hide this shit! Time may save me, but I hope you-- also do-- (laughter)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Killed. Buried beneath the surface, just to the point where he's pretty much flush with the ground of where it is. Contraption set, the objects placed back inside with some of the barrels and some of the leftover pieces of wood, the one overturned cart. You go ahead and take the chest and you place the glyph onto it. It takes a while to cast that spell, about an hour, but you complete it without an issue.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Go ranger, go ranger, go!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make a survival check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: "Which dice will I use? There's no way to choose."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I aid her in this bit of camouflage?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You want to sing me some inspiration?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Can I inspire her?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure, what do you want to sing?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (singing) R-r-ranger, turn and face the strange! R-r-ranger, looks like you're going to have to hide this shit! Time may save me, but I hope you-- also do-- (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 31. You guys take a good portion of the next hour or so, under her guidance and tutelage, hiding this entire trap, getting dirt, the top layer of sand to cover it so it looks like it hasn't been recently disturbed--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Little Dust of Tracelessness.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Little Dust of Tracelessness to fill in and smooth out any of the ripples of any possible passage that has come through. Fassbender has been-- (crash)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So. That being the case. You guys have your meals in the center of town, summoned forth a grand Heroes' Feast, two side by side. The chosen soldiers step up and you all begin to feast upon your meal. The taste is great but you're all distracted as the energies of the divine enchantment of these food items fill your body and steel you against the coming fear, there is still the concentration in your eye as you all look off listlessly in the distance as you finish your food. It's now just pushing past midnight.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I can also do a feast if I cast it at seventh level. But, fuck it, I'm going to sleep, so it doesn't matter.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You're going to do two feasts?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Pike and I are both going to cast Heroes' Feast, so that way we can feast 24 people total.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So tell Kevdak. Sorry, fuck! Tell Zanror his finest warriors, his druids--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And Abjurist Noja.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And Old Davin.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And his wife.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So make sure we have Zanror, his wife, his druids that he mentioned; there were like two to three druids?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Are there any magic-users in the room?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Magic-users or clerics.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Magic-users or clerics.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Abjurist and Old Davin included.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: What you're thinking of is Aid, and that lasts for eight hours. That's the one that gives the additional hit points he just mentioned.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Everyone seems to go and find themselves to rest, a tense sleep before them. Anything else you wish to do before you go to bed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Rise at sunrise? Rise at dawn?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Wait, before we do that, didn't you do something that gave us an extra amount of hit points before Kevdak?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I have Shield of Faith that I can put on somebody.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Will that last for 24 hours?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It's up to ten minutes. I can do it tomorrow.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Eight hours, yes, but a long rest is eight hours, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do you want to have it for the night?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, just in case he comes in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, when do we want to be at the trap? Dawn?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: At dawn!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just before.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, before first light.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He comes normally around noon, he said.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we need to be in our places by 5AM.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So each target's hit point maximum and current hit points increase by five for the duration.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For each level of the spell, so if you cast it at higher levels, it's more.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How high do you want to cast it?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Cast it at-- fifth is the highest that I have now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Rock out. Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Five times five?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll lose it when we wake up.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, do you want it for tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It'll hold for eight hours, right, or, but I lose it--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You run on a pretty consistent schedule, being a ranger.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So we need to be in our places by 5AM.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: So each target's hit point maximum and current hit points increase by five for the duration.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For each level of the spell, so if you cast it at higher levels, it's more.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: How high do you want to cast it?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Cast it at-- fifth is the highest that I have now.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Rock out. Go for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Five times five?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: That's, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You'll lose it when we wake up.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, do you want it for tonight?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It'll hold for eight hours, right, or, but I lose it--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Eight hours, yes, but a long rest is eight hours, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it'll go away. Let's wait.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time of year is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's winter cresting into spring--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Okay, so we have long nights right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We need to go the fuck to sleep.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: So let's sleep for a good six hours?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Six to eight hours.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Set our alarm clocks.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right, so the sun has come up, the hour hits to eight, nine in the morning, you're looking at about three hours until the dragon's--

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: To come help us kill a dragon. So that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Go ahead and roll a percentile dice, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What do I have--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll 2d10. You may have a die that has multiple numbers on it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I do. Is this the percentile?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yes. Roll that first. What'd you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: What do I need to roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I believe you need to roll under your level. What did you roll?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I rolled an 80.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It never works!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, as you call to Sarenrae, the boundaries and barriers that keep the gods from directly interfering with this realm are particularly strong this morning. While you feel her glow upon you, her warmth and protection guiding you to victory, there is not more within her strength or sphere of influence she can grant you at this time.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Dammit. I don't feel her warmth, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What else would you like to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Just go eat.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: In the trenches? All right, so you make your way into the trenches, pushing past all the rest of the Herd members.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can bards inspire themselves?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. At higher levels, he can.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Theoretically, I could enhance your ability, but it's a concentration spell and I've already protected from acid against Grog.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Never mind. I'm fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What does your cloak do? That I put on?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Disadvantage on attacking you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Like, melee?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Until you get hit, and then it's gone for the round but resets for the next round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Only melee, not spells, yeah?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, at this point in time, Kevdak approaches.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Zanror.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I did it again. Zanror and two of his goliaths stay there waiting, with the offering placed at its designated spot.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Shit. I can't believe we're doing this.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Any last-minute preparations?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to-- wait, Grog, where are you?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm down in the tunnel.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Is that where I am?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, wherever you want to be.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I want to be in the trenches.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Just so you guys are aware, this is over 100 points of damage in one round.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, surprise round doesn't give you advantage, but you were hidden from the top of the round, so you have advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay. So an additional 10d6 damage for the dragon arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Go ahead and roll damage on that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No. 6d10. Sorry, my mistake.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's going to one-shot the dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: 6d10. (counting)

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Matt, can I turn around and see Keyleth and Vax making out in the tree behind me?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: They are not.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're not making out! There's a slow trickle of wizz going down my leg right now, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm hyperventilating.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sorry. 24 plus ten is 34 plus Hunter's Mark is another four. 38 on that attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 38 points of damage on the dragon-slaying arrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And then my third attack, I'm going to use the Blazing Bowstring.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. It is now Noja's turn. Noja is going to step out from here and is going to attempt to Lightning Bolt. From the side, he just reaches out, hand forward. Arcs upward in its direction. It fails its saving throw because it is held in there. It is currently considered grappled. It doesn't have disadvantage or anything, but I'll say from this standpoint it can't really move around a lot. It's going to take the damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: With my bonus action, could I drink the potion of fast nuts? Potion of speed.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, isn't it a bonus action to go into a rage?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I believe you're right. Never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pretty sure it is. Let me double-check real fast.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I think you're right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's fine, yeah. Bonus action. So that's your turn? Percy, you're up. You're way behind the dragon, hiding by the treeside.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay, I'm going to cast Hex on the dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, Hex on the dragon.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: With a disadvantage to all strength checks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Strength ability checks, okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to hold my turn until the dragon's turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You're just going to hold until it does something?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to hold until it does something, and if it does nothing, I'm taking my attacks, and if it gets away I'm taking my attacks. I'm just taking my attacks after him.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. That brings us to Noja. Noja's going to attempt another Lightning Bolt. It's the best thing he has at the moment. Oh, a natural 20 on the saving throw. Nope. Not bad. 15 points of lightning damage to Umbrasyl. Noja's then going to dart back behind the tree. Ending Noja's turn, it is now Umbrasyl's turn. Umbrasyl is going to go ahead and attempt to break free of the final chains that are there.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Reduced to...

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Reduced to eight. Go ahead and make one more dexterity saving throw, just for my own sake.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 16. You are thrust off the pillar, pushed, falling, and hitting the ground, prone on the ground, the pillar remains there. You take an additional eight points of bludgeoning damage, reduced to four, from the impact. But you are knocked prone. The rest of the Herd here, we'll say for the sake of that, they do not save. Oh, man. They are all knocked prone, knocking much of the chains free from their hands as they're thrown onto their backs. Then it is still technically grappled, so it cannot fly half its speed, unfortunately. It does seem to have knocked a majority of the chains off its back. Percy, you're up.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Holding my action until its action.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Oh, I like to. I also want to point out that you guys held my dragon to the ground for two rounds and hit it like a goddamn pincushion.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That would be 7d6 radiant damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 22 points of damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: But I'll also-- never mind. We're good, 22.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You're good. Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And then I'm going to move my Spiritual Weapon.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, Spiritual Weapon, which is technically on the ground over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to move it to the back of his neck.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Let's see. Spiritual Weapon, can move it 20 feet and repeat the attack. It is more than 20 feet away.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's down there, so it can begin to shift upward in that direction. So we'll say that it's partway floating in midair.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Can it get his feet?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it can't. It's too far away, unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: All right, well, it'll start working its way up there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That ends your turn, Pike. All right. It would be the eagles' turn, but they got dissolved.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Thanks, Matt. Thank you for pointing that back out.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You're barely holding on up there, but yes. You take the brunt of it with your shield, and it almost knocks you off the side. You manage to take your sword and dig in to the opposite side and catch yourself on the edge of the pillar. It does not knock you off. Scanlan, it is now your turn. At the top of your turn, you take six points of acid damage, reduced to three, as you've found your way into the stomach. He did not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: And I'm going to take the remaining of my movement to move up--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Up?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Well, he has 60 feet of movement, but I moved, what, like ten feet earlier, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 60 feet of movement? His speed is 60?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm sorry. His Earthglide is-- Yes, 30, so he moves up 20 feet.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 20 feet, all right. That finishes your turn, Keyleth. Scanlan, you're up. Actually, at the end of Keyleth's turn, it's going to use a legendary action to use a tail attack against you, Pike, because you are within 20 feet of it. As it's in the air, its tail whips out toward you. That is a 32 to hit. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. Nope. No. Doesn't hit.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You take 22 points of bludgeoning damage.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: And I'm staying up there?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: His wife is over here. And when I say wrecked, they're on the ground, their flesh dissolving and screaming in pain. The Herd has been heavily culled in but a few rounds. There's a little bit of horror to it. I need both Grog and Keyleth as an earth elemental to make a dexterity saving throw.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. If the creature pushes against it, it must make a DC 30 strength check to move the rod up to ten feet in a single round. So. Nope.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That thing's fixed in space.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It goes to move and (grunts) and is feeling something tear through on the inside of its area so it stops with this sharp pain. Takes one point of damage, but the warning is there. It does not move. However, (laughs) the acid breath did recharge on a five. It's going to go ahead and go right there. Angrily lay down right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: No!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: No!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But you're resistant.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Isn't he having plummeting issues if he's not moving?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, if your speed is zero you plummet is how it works.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, they all fail. All right. They are all right over here. Wrecked. Wrecked. Wrecked. Wrecked.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where's Zanror?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Zanror's right there at the bottom.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Where's his wife?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's midnight!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's going to be a lot of damage, right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's a fuckin' missile.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That is 37 points of piercing damage as it ejects from the side. He is going to--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm still attached to him, too.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Correct. As you are pulled up, let's see here.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm going for a ride.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We burned through all of his legendary saves.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: One more.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're attached to him? By a chain?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yup.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You, Grog, are now being lifted up. Make a strength check.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Advantage on this. 24.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 24, yeah. Umbrasyl lifts up and goes full flying speed of 80 feet this way, carrying you dangling beneath and is going to go into a dash and continue to fly up towards Gatshadow.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (terrified laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He's scared.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: With Scanlan and Vax inside him still.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Work that broom, girl!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Grog dangling from beneath. And that's where we're going to pick up the next one. (yelling)

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Or you're going to have to wait two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's midnight!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Three of us are not here next week.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Three of you are not here-- That's right. Do you guys want to keep going then?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's either that or--

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Picking up in two weeks from now. (yelling)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's either that or--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Or you're going to have to wait two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're not here.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: What do you guys think? Would you rather wait two weeks?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I want to go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I mean, this is you guys, too. You guys have to stay.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You want to go, keep going?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It could be a long night.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Who's not here next week?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Laura, Travis, and Sam.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we're probably going to take off next week because we're missing half the party.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, so we'll just skip next week and come back in two weeks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, it's on us, because we have jobs and getting up in the morning.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we have work tomorrow.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Why don't we skip next week and come back in two weeks to finish?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like that's smarter.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right, let's do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're leaving me in this thing's anus for two weeks?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That 30 minutes fucked us.
